scenario.title: The Bank Loan Scandal
scenario.narrative: An AI loan officer approved a massive, illegal loan to a bankrupt applicant. The decision was: salary_sufficient OR has_guarantor. The defense pinned 4 evidence cards on the board and claims they achieved 100% Branch Condition coverage. Your job is to show that 100% BC can still hide an entire branch — the missing F-F case.

claim: Look at the Evidence Board! We tested Salary=True and Salary=False! We tested Guarantor=True and Guarantor=False! That is 100% Branch Condition coverage!
charges: The defense ran two tests — Test 1 (Salary=T, Guarantor=F) and Test 2 (Salary=F, Guarantor=T) — and claims full Branch Condition coverage.
Both conditions appear as TRUE and as FALSE somewhere across the suite, so BC is technically satisfied. But BCC demands the FULL 2^2 truth table, including the F-F row.
Connect two evidence cards whose FALSE values, when combined, reveal the untested F-F combination.
correct_answer_explanation: You synthesized the missing F-F card and slammed it on the Judge's bench! Splicing "Test 2: Salary = False" with "Test 1: Guarantor = False" reconstructs the (Salary=F, Guarantor=F) combination — a row that was NEVER executed. The Judge realizes that 100% BC coverage is a dangerous illusion: each variable was observed as T and F, yet the entire F-F branch was skipped. Only BCC, which demands all 2^N combinations, would have caught this gap.
wrong_answer_explanation: Those two clues don't reconstruct the missing combination. The defense already executed three of the four truth-table rows. Identify the one F-F combination that was never tested and connect the two FALSE evidence cards that point to it.
hints: Prosecutor's Note: The defense ran exactly two tests. List the four possible (Salary, Guarantor) combinations and check which one is absent from their evidence.
Apply BCC (ISO 29119-4 §5.3.5): every combination in the 2x2 truth table must appear in the suite. Which combination of FALSE values did the defense never run?
Worked solution: The missing row is (Salary=F, Guarantor=F). Connect "Test 2: Salary = False" with "Test 1: Guarantor = False" — both FALSE clues — to reconstruct that untested combination.

misconceptions.explanation_md: Branch Condition (BC) and Branch Condition Combination (BCC) are easy to confuse. **BC** only requires that each individual condition take both TRUE and FALSE values somewhere across the test suite — it can be satisfied by 2 tests for any predicate. **BCC** requires every cell of the 2^N truth table. As this case shows, a suite can hit 100% BC and still completely miss the F-F branch, leaving an entire equivalence class of inputs untested.
